title: CircuitBreaker HashMap grows unbounded; no eviction of healthy domains
description:
LOCATION: src/delivery.rs:27-57

FINDING: The CircuitBreaker struct holds a HashMap<String, (u32, Instant)> that only grows. record_success removes the entry (line 41), but domains that trip the breaker and then have the 1-hour cooldown expire are pruned lazily only when is_tripped is called for that specific domain (line 53). Domains that fail exactly 9 times (below the trip threshold of 10) accumulate in the map indefinitely — record_success is only called on success, so a domain that consistently fails at 9 attempts per window never gets cleared. On a server with many followers across many domains, the map grows proportional to the number of distinct failing domains, with no eviction for sub-threshold failure domains.

Also: the CircuitBreaker is in-memory inside the delivery worker and not shared with the rate limiter prune task. There is no periodic prune of the CircuitBreaker map.

IMPACT: Memory growth proportional to the number of domains that have had any delivery failure. On a server that has accumulated followers across thousands of domains (common for fediverse servers), the map grows to megabytes with no release mechanism. Not immediately catastrophic, but a silent memory leak for long-running instances.
close_reason: Fixed: CircuitBreaker prune removes entries >2h old at batch start
